threat.title: SQL Injection via Unparameterized Queries in Express.js
threat.description: When user input is directly concatenated into SQL queries without parameterization, attackers can inject malicious SQL code to manipulate queries, extract data, or execute administrative operations.

attack.scenario:
1. Attacker identifies user input field (e.g., username parameter)
2. Crafts SQL injection payload: admin' OR '1'='1
3. Application concatenates input directly into SQL query
4. Malicious query bypasses authentication or extracts data
5. Attacker gains unauthorized access or dumps entire database
prerequisites: Application uses SQL database (MySQL, PostgreSQL, MSSQL)
User input is concatenated into SQL queries
Application has database permissions for SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE

cve_references.description: SQL injection in vBulletin forum software

real_world_breaches.impact: 380,000 payment cards compromised, £20 million GDPR fine

bug_bounty_reports.summary: SQL injection leading to full database access

security_research.title: OWASP Top 10 2021 - Injection
security_research.key_finding: SQL injection remains in top 3 most critical web vulnerabilities

mitigations.title: Use Parameterized Queries (Prepared Statements)
mitigations.description: Always use parameterized queries where user input is passed as parameters, not concatenated into SQL strings.
validation_steps: Search codebase for string concatenation in SQL queries
Replace all concatenated queries with parameterized queries
Run SQL injection tests (e.g., sqlmap) against endpoints
Enable SQL query logging to verify parameterization

mitigations.title: Use ORM with Safe Query Builders
mitigations.description: Use Object-Relational Mapping (ORM) libraries like Sequelize or TypeORM that automatically parameterize queries.
validation_steps: Migrate raw SQL queries to ORM methods
Review all Sequelize.query() calls for parameterization

mitigations.title: Input Validation with Joi or express-validator
mitigations.description: Validate and sanitize all user input before using in queries (defense in depth).
validation_steps: Add validation to all user input endpoints
Define validation schemas for each endpoint

indicators: Unusual SQL errors in logs (syntax errors, quote escaping)
Database queries with suspicious characters (', --, /*, UNION)
Unexpected database access patterns (time-based delays, bulk exports)
WAF alerts for SQL injection patterns

log_queries.query:
// Detect SQL injection attempts
AppServiceHTTPLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(1h)
| where CsUriQuery contains "'" or CsUriQuery contains "--" or CsUriQuery contains "UNION"
| where ScStatus == 500 // Internal server error (SQL syntax error)
| summarize AttemptCount = count() by ClientIP, CsUriQuery
| where AttemptCount > 5
| order by AttemptCount desc

index=web_logs sourcetype=access_combined
| rex field=uri_query "(?<sqli_attempt>('|--|UNION|SELECT|INSERT|DROP))"
| where isnotnull(sqli_attempt)
| stats count by src_ip, sqli_attempt
| where count > 10

tools.name: OWASP ZAP
tools.usage: Automated scanning for SQL injection vulnerabilities
